BTC USA — independent comparison

Buy bitcoin in the United States

US-regulated crypto exchanges compared — FDIC-insured, tax-reporting friendly, and available in all 50 states.

–– options benchmarked Updated July 2026
View the comparison Read the guide

Buying crypto in the US means navigating a patchwork of state regulations, tax obligations, and platform restrictions. We've reviewed every major exchange available to American residents, focusing on regulatory compliance, state availability, fiat on-ramps, and tax reporting features.

01 Comparison

Best US crypto exchanges

Top pick

Coinbase

Largest US-regulated exchange

Publicly traded on NASDAQ, fully regulated in all 50 states, and the most trusted exchange for US users. FDIC-insured USD balances, 1099 tax reporting, and a beginner-friendly app.

  • Available in all 50 states,FDIC-insured USD deposits,1099 tax reporting,250+ supported cryptocurrencies,Coinbase One subscription for zero-fee trading
Pricing Maker 0.40% / Taker 0.60%
Visit Coinbase
Top pick

Kraken

Security-first US exchange

Operating since 2011 with zero security breaches. Kraken offers competitive fees, excellent customer support, and a wide range of trading pairs. Available in most US states.

  • Never been hacked,24/7 live support,Proof of reserves audits,Low maker/taker fees,Staking available (where permitted)
Pricing Maker 0.25% / Taker 0.40%
Visit Kraken
Top pick

Gemini

New York regulated & insured

Founded by the Winklevoss twins, Gemini is a New York trust company regulated by NYDFS. SOC 2 certified with insurance on custodied crypto. The go-to for security-conscious US investors.

  • NYDFS regulated trust company,SOC 2 Type 2 certified,Insured crypto custody,Gemini Credit Card with crypto rewards,ActiveTrader for advanced users
Pricing Maker 0.20% / Taker 0.40%
Visit Gemini
Top pick

Robinhood Crypto

Commission-free crypto trading

If you already use Robinhood for stocks, adding crypto is seamless. Zero commission trades, though the spread markup is higher than dedicated exchanges. Now supports transfers to external wallets.

  • Zero commission trades,Integrated with stocks and ETFs,Wallet transfers supported,Simple mobile-first interface,Available in most US states
Pricing $0 commission (spread markup applies)
Visit Robinhood
Top pick

Cash App

Easiest way to buy bitcoin

Cash App makes buying bitcoin as simple as sending money. Lightning Network support for instant, low-fee transfers. Limited to bitcoin only — no altcoins. Great for beginners who just want BTC.

  • Buy bitcoin in seconds,Lightning Network support,Auto-invest (recurring buys),Send bitcoin to any wallet,Free tax reporting with Cash App Taxes
Pricing Spread-based pricing (~1.5-2%)
Visit Cash App
02 Guide

Buying crypto in the US: what you need to know

Regulation matters

US-regulated exchanges register as Money Service Businesses with FinCEN and comply with state-level money transmitter licenses. Some states like New York require a separate BitLicense. Regulated exchanges provide 1099 tax forms and FDIC insurance on USD deposits.

Tax reporting

The IRS treats crypto as property. Every trade, swap, and sale is a taxable event. Choose an exchange that provides clear transaction history and integrates with tax software like TurboTax, CoinTracker, or Koinly.

Payment methods

ACH bank transfers are free on most US exchanges. Wire transfers are faster but cost $10-25. Debit/credit cards work but carry 2-4% fees. Some exchanges now support Apple Pay and PayPal.
